revision.text:
'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|ja|新田氏}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|ja|鳥羽天皇}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.
[[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:Kamakura-period sites]] [[Category:History of Ōta, Gunma]] [[ja:新田荘遺跡]]

'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Nitta-no-shō Iseki]] to [[Nitta Manor Ruins]]
revision.text:
'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|ja|新田氏}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|ja|鳥羽天皇}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.
[[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:Kamakura-period sites]] [[Category:History of Ōta, Gunma]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:新田荘遺跡]] [[ja:新田荘遺跡]]

'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|ja|新田氏}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|ja|鳥羽天皇}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town)

'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|ja|新田氏}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|ja|鳥羽天皇}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town)

'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|ja|新田氏}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|ja|鳥羽天皇}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.

== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.

revision.comment: /* Preservation as a National Historic Site */
revision.text:
'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|ja|新田氏}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|ja|鳥羽天皇}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref>
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.

revision.text:
'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|ja|新田氏}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|ja|鳥羽天皇}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.
[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamakura-period sites]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Ōta, Gunma]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:新田荘遺跡]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:11]] [[ja:新田荘遺跡]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:qq]]

'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Manor|ja|新田荘}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}'s {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District|ja|勢多郡}}, {{ill|Sai District|ja|佐位郡}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tonegawa River|ja|利根川}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|ja|新田氏}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|ja|鳥羽天皇}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.
[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamakura-period sites]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Ōta, Gunma]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:新田荘遺跡]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Manor Ruins}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:11]] [[ja:新田荘遺跡]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Manor Ruins]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
'''新田荘遺跡'''（にったのしょういせき）は、[[上野国]][[新田郡]]（[[群馬県]][[太田市]]）にあった[[荘園 (日本)|荘園]][[新田荘]]に関わる遺跡。荘域は上野国新田郡全域・[[勢多郡]]・[[佐位郡]]・[[武蔵国]][[榛沢郡]]の一部に及び、おもに[[大間々扇状地]]と[[利根川]]左岸[[氾濫原]]からなる田中大喜編『シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究　第3巻　上野新田氏』戎光祥出版　2011年6月　ISBN 978-4864030342。『[[延喜式]]』には上野国新田駅、『[[和名類聚抄]]』では新田郡駅家郷と記されている。中世[[武士団]][[新田氏]]一族の根源地として成立し、長楽寺文書、正木文書によって[[文献資料 (歴史学)|文献史料]]の裏付けを可能とする東国の中世荘園として稀有な例である。2000年（平成12年）、新田氏遺構群のうち11箇所が国の[[史跡]]に指定され、「新田荘遺跡」として保存されている。 == 新田荘の歴史 == * [[律令制]]下の郡として[[平安時代]]中期の承平年間([[931年]] - [[938年]])に作成された[[和名類聚抄|倭名抄]]に「新田郡」および[[東山道]]（[[東山道武蔵路|武蔵路]]も含む）の[[鉄道駅|駅]]として「新田駅」が記載される。 * [[1108年]]（天仁元年）の[[浅間山]]天仁大噴火によって火山灰（浅間Bテフラ）が降下して北関東一帯が荒廃。 * [[1142年]]（康治元年）- [[源義国]]、[[足利荘]]立券。 * [[1143年]]（康治2年）- 源義国、[[簗田御厨]]立券。 * [[1150年]]（久安6年）- 源義国、右近衛大将大炊御門藤原実能と争い、実能の屋敷を焼き払い勅勘を蒙り、源義国は下野国足利の別業に隠退する。 この後、源義国は長子・[[源義重|新田義重]]とともに上野国新田郡の開発を開始する。源義国・新田義重父子の開発過程で近隣の[[藤姓足利氏]]との間で相論が活発化していく。 * [[1155年]]（久寿2年）- 源義国が新田郡内の新田義重館にて死去。 * [[1157年]]（保元2年）- 源義国・義重父子によって開発された「空閑の郷々」19郷が[[本家]]に[[鳥羽天皇|鳥羽院]][[御願寺]]の[[金剛心院]]、[[領家]]に[[藤原氏]][[藤原北家|北家]][[花山院家|花山院流]][[藤原忠雅]]に[[寄進]]され、[[新田荘]]が立券。同年、藤原忠雅により新田義重が新田荘[[下司職]]に補任される。 * [[1170年]]（嘉応2年）- 新田荘域が新田郡全郡56郷に拡大。 * [[1172年]]（承安2年）- 新田義重、嫡子である[[新田義兼]]へ所領を譲渡。同年、新田義重、隣接する園田御厨と紛争を起こす。 * [[1193年]]（建久4年）- [[源頼朝]]、新田義重館へ遊覧。 * [[1215年]]（建保3年）- [[新田尼]]、新田荘内3郷の[[地頭|地頭職]]に、[[岩松時兼]]、同12郷の地頭職に補任される。 * [[1205年]]（元久2年）- 新田義兼が[[源実朝]]より村田新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領・田嶋・中今井・一井・田中世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領・堀口・多古宇・綿打・千歳・薮塚・上女塚・高嶋の12郷の地頭職に補任される。 * [[1221年]]（承久3年）- [[得川義季|世良田義季]]が開山に[[栄朝]]を招いて[[長楽寺 (太田市)|世良田長楽寺]]を開基。 * [[1226年]]（嘉禄2年）- [[岩松時兼]]、岩松郷の地頭職に補任される。 * [[1244年]]（寛元2年）- [[新田政義]]が京都大番役での上京中に幕府に無断で出家した罪で御家人役を剥奪される。新田氏[[惣領|惣領職]]惣領職に内包される新田氏一族は本宗系・世良田系・岩松系などであり、新田荘外に本拠を構え、独自に[[御家人]]役を勤仕し早期に独立した[[里見氏]]、[[山名氏]]は含まれないとする。は没収され、[[世良田頼氏]]に与えられる。[[世良田氏]]とともに新田荘半分領家職は[[岩松氏]]が分担する。この分担は、世良田氏が鎌倉で勤仕し、岩松氏が在地で領導していたとされる。 * [[1246年]]（寛元4年）- 世良田義季、女塚郷を長楽寺に寄進。 * [[1268年]]（文永9年）- [[得川頼有]]、[[岩松経兼]]の嫡子・亀王丸（[[岩松政経]]）を養子とし、所領を譲渡。 * [[1272年]]（文永9年）- 世良田頼氏が[[二月騒動]]に連座し[[佐渡国]]へ流罪となり、新田氏惣領職（世良田氏分）は新田氏本宗家（[[新田政氏]]）へ遷移する。 二月騒動を契機として新田荘内に[[得宗]]領が拡大していった。 * [[1277年]]（建治3年）- 世良田頼氏の娘「[[尼浄院]]」、上江田郷内堂垣内在家・田を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1291年]]（正応4年）- [[鎌倉幕府]]、[[鳥山時成]]の遺領相続を妻「[[尼念空]]」に認める。 * [[1318年]]（文保2年）- [[由良景長]]の妻[[紀氏]]に鎌倉幕府は上野国新田荘の田地を領せしむ。[[新田義貞]]が新田氏本宗家家督相続する。新田義貞・[[村田頼親]]が所領を長楽寺へ売却。 * [[1322年]]（元亨2年）- 岩松政経・[[大舘宗氏]]が新田荘田嶋郷の用水相論。「尼浄院」、亡父忌日料として南女塚村内在家を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1324年]]（元亨4年）- 新田義貞、所領を[[小此木盛光]]妻紀氏へ売却。 * [[1328年]]（嘉暦3年）- [[世良田満義]]、新田荘小角田村の田地等を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1332年]]（元弘3年）- [[大谷道海]]、火災にて焼失した長楽寺を再建。 * [[1333年]]（元弘3年）- 新田義貞が倒幕の挙兵。[[江田行義]]、所領を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1334年]]（建武元年）- [[妙連]]（土用王御前）[[相馬重胤]]娘、岩松時兼妻、所領を養子・[[岩松直国]]岩松政経の子・[[岩松経家]]の嫡子へ譲渡。 * [[1335年]]（建武2年）- [[足利尊氏]]、東国にある新田氏一族の所領を没収。 * [[1338年]]（暦応元年）- 足利尊氏、新田氏の旧領を長楽寺へ寄進。足利尊氏、新田荘八木沼郷等を安養寺殿（新田義貞）追善料所として長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1347年]]（貞和3年）- 岩松直国、新田荘由良郷地頭職に補任される。 * [[1350年]]（観応元年）- [[室町幕府]]、[[上杉憲顕]]に命じ、新田荘内の[[世良田右京亮]]・[[桃井直常]]らの旧領を岩松直国に与える。 * [[1352年]]（観応3年）- [[新田義興]]、長楽寺に兵士の乱入狼藉するを停止する。足利尊氏、長楽寺の要請により、寺領安堵の署判を与える。 * [[1355年]]（文和4年）- [[村田頼氏]]、下江田郷内在家・田畠を売却。 * [[1364年]]（貞治3年）- [[足利基氏]]、[[世良田義政]]跡の新田荘江田郷を岩松直国に与える。 * [[1365年]]（貞治4年）- [[足利義詮]]、岩松直国に本領安堵の御教書を与える。 * [[1381年]]（永徳元年）- [[世良田憲政]]、所領を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1411年]]（応永18年）- [[鎌倉府]]、[[岩松満国]]に新田荘当年年貢百貫文を沙汰させる。 [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝の動乱]]を経て、新田氏本宗家の滅亡後、新田氏惣領職は岩松氏新田荘に残存した新田氏一族は岩松氏惣領家を中心に再編されたと考えられる。に、新田荘領家職を鎌倉府に与えられる。岩松氏は[[守護職]]とほぼ同様の権力[[守護領国制]]・[[一円知行]]を参照を新田郡に有したと考えられる。 * [[1416年]]（応永23年）- [[岩松満純]]が[[上杉禅秀の乱]]に加担し、以後、岩松氏は礼部家と京兆家に分裂。 * [[1455年]]（享徳4年）- [[岩松持国]]、[[足利成氏]]に新田荘の一元的支配権の承認を要求する。 * [[1469年]]（文明元年）- [[岩松家純]]（礼部家）によって岩松氏は統一される。同年、岩松家純によって[[新田金山城]]が築城される。 * [[1495年]]（明応4年）- [[被官]][[横瀬景繁]]の専横により[[岩松尚純]]が隠居し、[[岩松昌純]]が擁立される。 この後、横瀬景繁の子・[[横瀬泰繁]]が岩松昌純を殺害する[[下克上]]を行い、[[横瀬氏|由良氏（横瀬氏）]]は[[戦国大名]]化する。 * [[1509年]]（永正6年）- [[連歌師]][[宗長]]、新田荘へ来着して岩松尚純と百韻連歌を行う。 * [[1590年]]（天正18年）- [[小田原征伐]]の後、[[徳川家康]]が関東入国し、[[太閤検地]]によって名実共に[[荘園 (日本)|荘園制]]が崩壊する。 * [[2000年]]（平成12年）- '''新田荘遺跡'''が国の史跡に指定される。 == 荘域 == ;1157年の「空閑の郷々」19郷「『新田義重置文』正木文書3」 {{columns-list|colwidth=15em| *女塚「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領 *上江田 *下江田世良田氏庶流の[[得川頼有]]（下野守）が、得川・横瀬・下江田などを[[岩松経兼]]に嫁した娘「女子源氏」を通じて[[岩松政経]]（下野太郎）に譲渡した。 *田中 *大館 *粕川 *小角 *押切 *出塚 *世良田 *三木 *上今井 *下今井 *上平塚 *下平塚 *木崎 *長福寺 *多古宇 *八木沼 }} ;1170年の荘域[[1170年]]（嘉応2年）『上野新田荘嘉応年中目録』
（[[1455年]]（享徳4年）「新田荘田畠在家注文」（正木文書83）[[岩松持国]]が[[足利成氏]]に新田荘支配の正統性を主張するために提出した嘉応2年当時の荘域を示す史料） :北限：勢多郡新里（[[桐生市]]/鹿田山）須永御厨との境界 :南限：武蔵国[[榛沢郡]]横瀬（[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]/[[利根川]]・[[広瀬川 (群馬県)|広瀬川]]の流路・[[小山川]]左岸：近世以前の利根川旧河道） :東限：飯塚（太田市/金山丘陵、蛇川・八瀬川の流路）薗田御厨・梁田御厨・藤姓足利荘・源姓足利荘との境界 :西限：佐位郡木島（[[伊勢崎市]]/早川の流路）淵名荘との境界 {{columns-list|colwidth=15em| *太田里見氏に伝領 *田嶋 *東牛澤 *岩松 *成塚 *一井 *薮塚 *額戸 *青根 *西牛澤 *濱田 *二児塚 *鶴生田 *石塩（中岡） *大嶋 *由良 *今井 *村田 *高林（竹林） *長手 *新川注釈に『さかいにとらる』とあり、1455年（享徳4年）当時、近隣に押妨されていた *鹿田 *北鹿田 *阿佐見 *小泉 *足垂 *藤心 *高嶋 *飯塚 *岩瀬川 *綿打注釈に『さかいに半ハとらる』とあり、1455年（享徳4年）当時、近隣に押妨されていた *田部井 *はなか塚 *女塚 *木嶋 *菱嶋 *千歳 *横瀬 *小倉 }} == 史跡新田荘遺跡 == 新田氏遺構群のうち下記の11箇所が'''新田荘遺跡'''として国の史跡に指定されている。 *円福寺境内（太田市別所町） *十二所神社境内（太田市別所町） *総持寺境内（太田市世良田町） *[[長楽寺 (太田市)|長楽寺]]境内（太田市世良田町） *[[世良田東照宮|東照宮]]境内（太田市世良田町） *明王院境内（太田市安養寺町） *[[生品神社]]境内（太田市新田市野井町） *[[反町薬師|反町館跡]]（太田市新田反町） *江田館跡（太田市新田上江田町） *重殿水源（太田市新田市野井町） *矢太神水源（太田市新田大根町） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[千々和実]]編『新田氏根本史料』（旧版『新田義貞公根本史料』群馬県教育会） * 群馬県史 資料編5 中世I 古文書・古記録 * 新田町史 第4巻 特集編 新田荘と新田氏 * [[峰岸純夫]]「新田荘」（[[網野善彦]]・[[石井進]]・[[稲垣泰彦]]・[[永原慶二]]編『講座日本荘園史5　東北・関東・東海地方の荘園』吉川弘文館　1990年5月1日　ISBN 9784642026956） * 峰岸純夫「東国武士の基盤」（稲垣泰彦編『荘園の世界』東京大学出版会） * 峰岸純夫「室町時代東国における領主の存在形態」（『史学』34巻4・5合併号） * 峰岸純夫『中世の東国―地域と権力』東京大学出版会　1989年4月25日　ISBN 9784130200912 * [[小山靖憲]] 「東国における領主制と村落―上野国新田荘を中心に」（『史潮』94号・同著『中世村落と荘園絵図 』東京大学出版会 1987年11月16日　ISBN 9784130200783　所収） * [[黒田日出男]]「新田荘成立期の郷と在家―『嘉応二年目録』の検討―」（体系日本史叢書　土地制度史I』月報17号　山川出版社） * [[山本隆志]] ミネルヴァ日本評伝選『新田義貞』 * [[田中大喜]]編『シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究　第3巻　上野新田氏』戎光祥出版　2011年6月　ISBN 9784864030342 * 山本隆志「新田氏成立の政治過程－源義国の政治史」（『近藤義雄卒寿記念論文集』（群馬県文化振興財団、2010年/改題所収:「北関東における武士勢力成立の政治史－新田氏成立の政治史的考察－」山本『東国における武士勢力の成立と発展』（[[思文閣出版]]、2012年） ISBN 978-4-7842-1601-7） * 久保田順一『中世武士選書18　新田義重　北関東の治承・寿永内乱』戎光祥出版 2013年11月1日 ISBN 978-4-86403-094-6 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の荘園の一覧]] * [[関東地方の史跡一覧]] * 日根荘遺跡 - [[慈眼院]] * [[田染荘]] * [[骨寺村荘園遺跡]] * [[太田市立新田荘歴史資料館]] * [[八幡荘 (上野国)]] * [[平賀氏]] * [[金津氏]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース] * [http://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/watarase/rekishi/02_2.html 新田荘の開発（関東農政局）] * [http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka54.html 新田荘遺跡（太田市教育委員会）] * [http://www.ota-kanko.jp/history/kunishiteishiseki.htm 国指定新田荘遺跡（太田市観光協会）] * [https://sanadada.com/4654/ 徳川家康・先祖の地「新田荘遺跡」の解説～「徳川氏発祥の地」の遺跡を探る～] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:につたのしよういせき}} [[Category:上野国]] [[Category:群馬県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:新田氏|跡]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の遺跡]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[新田荘遺跡]] to [[Nitta Manor Ruins]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''新田荘遺跡'''（にったのしょういせき）は、[[上野国]][[新田郡]]（[[群馬県]][[太田市]]）にあった[[荘園 (日本)|荘園]][[新田荘]]に関わる遺跡。荘域は上野国新田郡全域・[[勢多郡]]・[[佐位郡]]・[[武蔵国]][[榛沢郡]]の一部に及び、おもに[[大間々扇状地]]と[[利根川]]左岸[[氾濫原]]からなる田中大喜編『シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究　第3巻　上野新田氏』戎光祥出版　2011年6月　ISBN 978-4864030342。『[[延喜式]]』には上野国新田駅、『[[和名類聚抄]]』では新田郡駅家郷と記されている。中世[[武士団]][[新田氏]]一族の根源地として成立し、長楽寺文書、正木文書によって[[文献資料 (歴史学)|文献史料]]の裏付けを可能とする東国の中世荘園として稀有な例である。2000年（平成12年）、新田氏遺構群のうち11箇所が国の[[史跡]]に指定され、「新田荘遺跡」として保存されている。 == 新田荘の歴史 == * [[律令制]]下の郡として[[平安時代]]中期の承平年間([[931年]] - [[938年]])に作成された[[和名類聚抄|倭名抄]]に「新田郡」および[[東山道]]（[[東山道武蔵路|武蔵路]]も含む）の[[鉄道駅|駅]]として「新田駅」が記載される。 * [[1108年]]（天仁元年）の[[浅間山]]天仁大噴火によって火山灰（浅間Bテフラ）が降下して北関東一帯が荒廃。 * [[1142年]]（康治元年）- [[源義国]]、[[足利荘]]立券。 * [[1143年]]（康治2年）- 源義国、[[簗田御厨]]立券。 * [[1150年]]（久安6年）- 源義国、右近衛大将大炊御門藤原実能と争い、実能の屋敷を焼き払い勅勘を蒙り、源義国は下野国足利の別業に隠退する。 この後、源義国は長子・[[源義重|新田義重]]とともに上野国新田郡の開発を開始する。源義国・新田義重父子の開発過程で近隣の[[藤姓足利氏]]との間で相論が活発化していく。 * [[1155年]]（久寿2年）- 源義国が新田郡内の新田義重館にて死去。 * [[1157年]]（保元2年）- 源義国・義重父子によって開発された「空閑の郷々」19郷が[[本家]]に[[鳥羽天皇|鳥羽院]][[御願寺]]の[[金剛心院]]、[[領家]]に[[藤原氏]][[藤原北家|北家]][[花山院家|花山院流]][[藤原忠雅]]に[[寄進]]され、[[新田荘]]が立券。同年、藤原忠雅により新田義重が新田荘[[下司職]]に補任される。 * [[1170年]]（嘉応2年）- 新田荘域が新田郡全郡56郷に拡大。 * [[1172年]]（承安2年）- 新田義重、嫡子である[[新田義兼]]へ所領を譲渡。同年、新田義重、隣接する園田御厨と紛争を起こす。 * [[1193年]]（建久4年）- [[源頼朝]]、新田義重館へ遊覧。 * [[1215年]]（建保3年）- [[新田尼]]、新田荘内3郷の[[地頭|地頭職]]に、[[岩松時兼]]、同12郷の地頭職に補任される。 * [[1205年]]（元久2年）- 新田義兼が[[源実朝]]より村田新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領・田嶋・中今井・一井・田中世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領・堀口・多古宇・綿打・千歳・薮塚・上女塚・高嶋の12郷の地頭職に補任される。 * [[1221年]]（承久3年）- [[得川義季|世良田義季]]が開山に[[栄朝]]を招いて[[長楽寺 (太田市)|世良田長楽寺]]を開基。 * [[1226年]]（嘉禄2年）- [[岩松時兼]]、岩松郷の地頭職に補任される。 * [[1244年]]（寛元2年）- [[新田政義]]が京都大番役での上京中に幕府に無断で出家した罪で御家人役を剥奪される。新田氏[[惣領|惣領職]]惣領職に内包される新田氏一族は本宗系・世良田系・岩松系などであり、新田荘外に本拠を構え、独自に[[御家人]]役を勤仕し早期に独立した[[里見氏]]、[[山名氏]]は含まれないとする。は没収され、[[世良田頼氏]]に与えられる。[[世良田氏]]とともに新田荘半分領家職は[[岩松氏]]が分担する。この分担は、世良田氏が鎌倉で勤仕し、岩松氏が在地で領導していたとされる。 * [[1246年]]（寛元4年）- 世良田義季、女塚郷を長楽寺に寄進。 * [[1268年]]（文永9年）- [[得川頼有]]、[[岩松経兼]]の嫡子・亀王丸（[[岩松政経]]）を養子とし、所領を譲渡。 * [[1272年]]（文永9年）- 世良田頼氏が[[二月騒動]]に連座し[[佐渡国]]へ流罪となり、新田氏惣領職（世良田氏分）は新田氏本宗家（[[新田政氏]]）へ遷移する。 二月騒動を契機として新田荘内に[[得宗]]領が拡大していった。 * [[1277年]]（建治3年）- 世良田頼氏の娘「[[尼浄院]]」、上江田郷内堂垣内在家・田を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1291年]]（正応4年）- [[鎌倉幕府]]、[[鳥山時成]]の遺領相続を妻「[[尼念空]]」に認める。 * [[1318年]]（文保2年）- [[由良景長]]の妻[[紀氏]]に鎌倉幕府は上野国新田荘の田地を領せしむ。[[新田義貞]]が新田氏本宗家家督相続する。新田義貞・[[村田頼親]]が所領を長楽寺へ売却。 * [[1322年]]（元亨2年）- 岩松政経・[[大舘宗氏]]が新田荘田嶋郷の用水相論。「尼浄院」、亡父忌日料として南女塚村内在家を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1324年]]（元亨4年）- 新田義貞、所領を[[小此木盛光]]妻紀氏へ売却。 * [[1328年]]（嘉暦3年）- [[世良田満義]]、新田荘小角田村の田地等を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1332年]]（元弘3年）- [[大谷道海]]、火災にて焼失した長楽寺を再建。 * [[1333年]]（元弘3年）- 新田義貞が倒幕の挙兵。[[江田行義]]、所領を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1334年]]（建武元年）- [[妙連]]（土用王御前）[[相馬重胤]]娘、岩松時兼妻、所領を養子・[[岩松直国]]岩松政経の子・[[岩松経家]]の嫡子へ譲渡。 * [[1335年]]（建武2年）- [[足利尊氏]]、東国にある新田氏一族の所領を没収。 * [[1338年]]（暦応元年）- 足利尊氏、新田氏の旧領を長楽寺へ寄進。足利尊氏、新田荘八木沼郷等を安養寺殿（新田義貞）追善料所として長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1347年]]（貞和3年）- 岩松直国、新田荘由良郷地頭職に補任される。 * [[1350年]]（観応元年）- [[室町幕府]]、[[上杉憲顕]]に命じ、新田荘内の[[世良田右京亮]]・[[桃井直常]]らの旧領を岩松直国に与える。 * [[1352年]]（観応3年）- [[新田義興]]、長楽寺に兵士の乱入狼藉するを停止する。足利尊氏、長楽寺の要請により、寺領安堵の署判を与える。 * [[1355年]]（文和4年）- [[村田頼氏]]、下江田郷内在家・田畠を売却。 * [[1364年]]（貞治3年）- [[足利基氏]]、[[世良田義政]]跡の新田荘江田郷を岩松直国に与える。 * [[1365年]]（貞治4年）- [[足利義詮]]、岩松直国に本領安堵の御教書を与える。 * [[1381年]]（永徳元年）- [[世良田憲政]]、所領を長楽寺へ寄進。 * [[1411年]]（応永18年）- [[鎌倉府]]、[[岩松満国]]に新田荘当年年貢百貫文を沙汰させる。 [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝の動乱]]を経て、新田氏本宗家の滅亡後、新田氏惣領職は岩松氏新田荘に残存した新田氏一族は岩松氏惣領家を中心に再編されたと考えられる。に、新田荘領家職を鎌倉府に与えられる。岩松氏は[[守護職]]とほぼ同様の権力[[守護領国制]]・[[一円知行]]を参照を新田郡に有したと考えられる。 * [[1416年]]（応永23年）- [[岩松満純]]が[[上杉禅秀の乱]]に加担し、以後、岩松氏は礼部家と京兆家に分裂。 * [[1455年]]（享徳4年）- [[岩松持国]]、[[足利成氏]]に新田荘の一元的支配権の承認を要求する。 * [[1469年]]（文明元年）- [[岩松家純]]（礼部家）によって岩松氏は統一される。同年、岩松家純によって[[新田金山城]]が築城される。 * [[1495年]]（明応4年）- [[被官]][[横瀬景繁]]の専横により[[岩松尚純]]が隠居し、[[岩松昌純]]が擁立される。 この後、横瀬景繁の子・[[横瀬泰繁]]が岩松昌純を殺害する[[下克上]]を行い、[[横瀬氏|由良氏（横瀬氏）]]は[[戦国大名]]化する。 * [[1509年]]（永正6年）- [[連歌師]][[宗長]]、新田荘へ来着して岩松尚純と百韻連歌を行う。 * [[1590年]]（天正18年）- [[小田原征伐]]の後、[[徳川家康]]が関東入国し、[[太閤検地]]によって名実共に[[荘園 (日本)|荘園制]]が崩壊する。 * [[2000年]]（平成12年）- '''新田荘遺跡'''が国の史跡に指定される。 == 荘域 == ;1157年の「空閑の郷々」19郷「『新田義重置文』正木文書3」 {{columns-list|colwidth=15em| *女塚「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領 *上江田 *下江田世良田氏庶流の[[得川頼有]]（下野守）が、得川・横瀬・下江田などを[[岩松経兼]]に嫁した娘「女子源氏」を通じて[[岩松政経]]（下野太郎）に譲渡した。 *田中 *大館 *粕川 *小角 *押切 *出塚 *世良田 *三木 *上今井 *下今井 *上平塚 *下平塚 *木崎 *長福寺 *多古宇 *八木沼 }} ;1170年の荘域[[1170年]]（嘉応2年）『上野新田荘嘉応年中目録』
（[[1455年]]（享徳4年）「新田荘田畠在家注文」（正木文書83）[[岩松持国]]が[[足利成氏]]に新田荘支配の正統性を主張するために提出した嘉応2年当時の荘域を示す史料） :北限：勢多郡新里（[[桐生市]]/鹿田山）須永御厨との境界 :南限：武蔵国[[榛沢郡]]横瀬（[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]/[[利根川]]・[[広瀬川 (群馬県)|広瀬川]]の流路・[[小山川]]左岸：近世以前の利根川旧河道） :東限：飯塚（太田市/金山丘陵、蛇川・八瀬川の流路）薗田御厨・梁田御厨・藤姓足利荘・源姓足利荘との境界 :西限：佐位郡木島（[[伊勢崎市]]/早川の流路）淵名荘との境界 {{columns-list|colwidth=15em| *太田里見氏に伝領 *田嶋 *東牛澤 *岩松 *成塚 *一井 *薮塚 *額戸 *青根 *西牛澤 *濱田 *二児塚 *鶴生田 *石塩（中岡） *大嶋 *由良 *今井 *村田 *高林（竹林） *長手 *新川注釈に『さかいにとらる』とあり、1455年（享徳4年）当時、近隣に押妨されていた *鹿田 *北鹿田 *阿佐見 *小泉 *足垂 *藤心 *高嶋 *飯塚 *岩瀬川 *綿打注釈に『さかいに半ハとらる』とあり、1455年（享徳4年）当時、近隣に押妨されていた *田部井 *はなか塚 *女塚 *木嶋 *菱嶋 *千歳 *横瀬 *小倉 }} == 史跡新田荘遺跡 == 新田氏遺構群のうち下記の11箇所が'''新田荘遺跡'''として国の史跡に指定されている。 *円福寺境内（太田市別所町） *十二所神社境内（太田市別所町） *総持寺境内（太田市世良田町） *[[長楽寺 (太田市)|長楽寺]]境内（太田市世良田町） *[[世良田東照宮|東照宮]]境内（太田市世良田町） *明王院境内（太田市安養寺町） *[[生品神社]]境内（太田市新田市野井町） *[[反町薬師|反町館跡]]（太田市新田反町） *江田館跡（太田市新田上江田町） *重殿水源（太田市新田市野井町） *矢太神水源（太田市新田大根町） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[千々和実]]編『新田氏根本史料』（旧版『新田義貞公根本史料』群馬県教育会） * 群馬県史 資料編5 中世I 古文書・古記録 * 新田町史 第4巻 特集編 新田荘と新田氏 * [[峰岸純夫]]「新田荘」（[[網野善彦]]・[[石井進]]・[[稲垣泰彦]]・[[永原慶二]]編『講座日本荘園史5　東北・関東・東海地方の荘園』吉川弘文館　1990年5月1日　ISBN 9784642026956） * 峰岸純夫「東国武士の基盤」（稲垣泰彦編『荘園の世界』東京大学出版会） * 峰岸純夫「室町時代東国における領主の存在形態」（『史学』34巻4・5合併号） * 峰岸純夫『中世の東国―地域と権力』東京大学出版会　1989年4月25日　ISBN 9784130200912 * [[小山靖憲]] 「東国における領主制と村落―上野国新田荘を中心に」（『史潮』94号・同著『中世村落と荘園絵図 』東京大学出版会 1987年11月16日　ISBN 9784130200783　所収） * [[黒田日出男]]「新田荘成立期の郷と在家―『嘉応二年目録』の検討―」（体系日本史叢書　土地制度史I』月報17号　山川出版社） * [[山本隆志]] ミネルヴァ日本評伝選『新田義貞』 * [[田中大喜]]編『シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究　第3巻　上野新田氏』戎光祥出版　2011年6月　ISBN 9784864030342 * 山本隆志「新田氏成立の政治過程－源義国の政治史」（『近藤義雄卒寿記念論文集』（群馬県文化振興財団、2010年/改題所収:「北関東における武士勢力成立の政治史－新田氏成立の政治史的考察－」山本『東国における武士勢力の成立と発展』（[[思文閣出版]]、2012年） ISBN 978-4-7842-1601-7） * 久保田順一『中世武士選書18　新田義重　北関東の治承・寿永内乱』戎光祥出版 2013年11月1日 ISBN 978-4-86403-094-6 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の荘園の一覧]] * [[関東地方の史跡一覧]] * 日根荘遺跡 - [[慈眼院]] * [[田染荘]] * [[骨寺村荘園遺跡]] * [[太田市立新田荘歴史資料館]] * [[八幡荘 (上野国)]] * [[平賀氏]] * [[金津氏]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース] * [http://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/watarase/rekishi/02_2.html 新田荘の開発（関東農政局）] * [http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka54.html 新田荘遺跡（太田市教育委員会）] * [http://www.ota-kanko.jp/history/kunishiteishiseki.htm 国指定新田荘遺跡（太田市観光協会）] * [https://sanadada.com/4654/ 徳川家康・先祖の地「新田荘遺跡」の解説～「徳川氏発祥の地」の遺跡を探る～] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:につたのしよういせき}} [[Category:上野国]] [[Category:群馬県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:新田氏|跡]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の遺跡]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]]

revision.text:
'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to [[:en:Nitta Shōen|Nitta Manor]], a shōen (estate) in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]'s [[:en:Nitta District, Gunma|Nitta District]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|qq=}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|qq=}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|qq=}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|qq=}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|qq=}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.

{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>

'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to [[:en:Nitta Shōen|Nitta Manor]], a shōen (estate) in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]'s [[:en:Nitta District, Gunma|Nitta District]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|qq=}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|qq=}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|qq=}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture
revision.text:
'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to [[:en:Nitta Shōen|Nitta Manor]], a shōen (estate) in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]'s [[:en:Nitta District, Gunma|Nitta District]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|qq=}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|qq=}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|qq=}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|qq=}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|qq=}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|qq=}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|qq=}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.
[[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|新田荘遺跡|version=99487096|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11503769]] Wikidata name: Nitta-no-shō National Historical Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to [[:en:Nitta Shōen|Nitta Manor]], a shōen (estate) in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]'s [[:en:Nitta District, Gunma|Nitta District]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.
