<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11503769]] Wikidata name: Nitta-no-shō National Historical Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to [[:en:Nitta Shōen|Nitta Manor]], a shōen (estate) in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]'s [[:en:Nitta District, Gunma|Nitta District]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.
[[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|新田荘遺跡|version=99487096|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11503769]] Wikidata name: Nitta-no-shō National Historical Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to [[:en:Nitta Shōen|Nitta Manor]], a shōen (estate) in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]'s [[:en:Nitta District, Gunma|Nitta District]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11503769]] Wikidata name: Nitta-no-shō National Historical Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to [[:en:Nitta Shōen|Nitta Manor]], a shōen (estate) in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]'s [[:en:Nitta District, Gunma|Nitta District]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11503769]] Wikidata name: Nitta-no-shō National Historical Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to [[:en:Nitta Shōen|Nitta Manor]], a shōen (estate) in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]'s [[:en:Nitta District, Gunma|Nitta District]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of [[:en:Seta District, Gunma|Seta District]], [[:en:Sai District, Gunma|Sai District]], and portions of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the [[:en:Tone River|Tonegawa River]].<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]] lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the [[:en:Nitta clan|Nitta clan]], a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the [[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]], compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]], with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above.
{{translated page|ja|新田荘遺跡|version=99487096|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| }}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11503769]] Wikidata name: Nitta-no-shō National Historical Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Shōen|en|Nitta Shōen|WD=Q24897972|lt=Nitta Manor}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}}'s {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District, Gunma|en|Seta District, Gunma|WD=Q2324302|lt=Seta District}}, {{ill|Sai District, Gunma|en|Sai District, Gunma|WD=Q11382916|lt=Sai District}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tonegawa River}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|en|Nitta clan|WD=Q2274150|lt=Nitta clan}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above. {{translated page|ja|新田荘遺跡|version=99487096|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:新田荘遺跡]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11503769}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Kamakura archaeological sites]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above. {{translated page|ja|新田荘遺跡|version=99487096|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]]
'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Shōen|en|Nitta Shōen|WD=Q24897972|lt=Nitta Manor}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}}'s {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District, Gunma|en|Seta District, Gunma|WD=Q2324302|lt=Seta District}}, {{ill|Sai District, Gunma|en|Sai District, Gunma|WD=Q11382916|lt=Sai District}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tonegawa River}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|en|Nitta clan|WD=Q2274150|lt=Nitta clan}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Chijiwa Minoru|ja|千々和実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijiwa Minoru|1=Minoru Chijiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213655}}, ed., *Root Historical Sources of the Nitta Clan*, Gunma Prefecture Educational Society. * Gunma Prefecture Historical Resources, Vol. 5: Medieval I - Ancient Records and Documents. * {{ill|Minegishi Jun'ō|ja|峰岸純夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegishi Jun'ō|1=Sumio Minegishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476142}}, *The Foundations of the Eastern Samurai in Medieval Japan*. * {{ill|Kobayashi Seiken|ja|小山靖憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Seiken|1=Yasunori Koyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460013}}, *The Manor System and Village Communities of Eastern Japan*. * {{ill|Tanaka Daiki|ja|田中大喜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Daiki|1=Hiroki Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17686940}}, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*. * Additional references included in the full text above. {{translated page|ja|新田荘遺跡|version=99487096|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:新田荘遺跡]]

== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田荘遺跡]]
'''Nitta-no-shō Iseki''' (新田荘遺跡) refers to the archaeological sites related to {{ill|Nitta Shōen|en|Nitta Shōen|WD=Q24897972|lt=Nitta Manor|1=Nitta Shōen}}, a shōen (estate) in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}'s {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta|1=Ōta, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. The estate encompassed the entire Nitta District, parts of {{ill|Seta District, Gunma|en|Seta District, Gunma|WD=Q2324302|lt=Seta District|1=Seta District, Gunma}}, {{ill|Sai District, Gunma|en|Sai District, Gunma|WD=Q11382916|lt=Sai District|1=Sai District, Gunma}}, and portions of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}'s {{ill|Hanzawa District|ja|榛沢郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzawa District|1=Hanzawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541893}}, mainly comprising the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}} and the floodplain of the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tonegawa River|1=Tone River}}.<ref>Tanaka Daiki, ed., *Series: Research on Medieval Samurai of Kantō, Vol. 3: The Nitta Clan of Kōzuke*, Yoshikōshō Publishing, June 2011, ISBN 978-4864030342.</ref> The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} mentions "Nitta Station" in Kōzuke Province, and the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}} lists "Nitta District's Ekiya-gō." Established as the ancestral land of the {{ill|Nitta clan|en|Nitta clan|WD=Q2274150|lt=Nitta clan|1=Nitta clan}}, a samurai family of medieval Japan, Nitta Manor is unique among eastern shōen for its detailed documentation in historical records such as the {{ill|Chōrakuji Documents|ja|長楽寺文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōrakuji Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the Masaki Documents. In 2000 (Heisei 12), eleven locations associated with the Nitta clan's heritage were designated as National Historic Sites under the title "Nitta-no-shō Iseki."
== History of Nitta Manor == * In the mid-Heian period, the district is mentioned in the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938), noting "Nitta District" and "Nitta Station" along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}} route, including the Musashi Road. * 1108 (Ten'nin 1): The major eruption of Mount Asama deposited ash (Asama B Tephra) across northern Kantō, devastating the region. * 1142 (Kōji 1): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Ashikaga Manor. * 1143 (Kōji 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni establishes the Yanada Mikuriya. * 1150 (Kyūan 6): Minamoto no Yoshikuni burns down the residence of Fujiwara no Saneyoshi, the Grand Minister of the Right, and retreats to the Ashikaga estate in Shimotsuke Province after being exiled by imperial decree. Subsequently, Yoshikuni begins developing the Nitta District with his eldest son, {{ill|Nitta Yoshishige|ja|新田義重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta Yoshishige|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義重|1=Minamoto no Yoshishige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q732097}}. * 1155 (Kyūju 2): Minamoto no Yoshikuni dies at the Yoshishige residence in Nitta District. * 1157 (Hōgen 2): Nineteen settlements in the "unclaimed villages" developed by Yoshikuni and Yoshishige are donated to {{ill|Kongōshin-in|ja|金剛心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongōshin-in|1=Kongōshin-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646536}}, the imperial wish temple of {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba|1=Emperor Toba}}, with {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadazane|ja|藤原忠雅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadazane|1=Fujiwara no Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623265}} acting as estate proprietor. Yoshishige is appointed administrator of Nitta Manor. * 1170 (Kaō 2): Nitta Manor expands to encompass all 56 settlements of Nitta District. * For a detailed timeline, see full article text above.
== Area of Nitta Manor == ; Nineteen "unclaimed villages" in 1157<ref>"Nitta Yoshishige's Testament," Masaki Documents 3.</ref>
{{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Onnazuka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamigoeda * Shimogoeda<ref>Inherited by the Iwamatsu clan via daughters of Tokugawa Yoriyasu.</ref> * Tanaka<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Ōdate * Kasukawa * Kosumi * Oshikiri<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Deizuka * Serada<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Mikki<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kamimai<ref name=nitaama/> * Shimomai<ref name=raiouhaha/> * Kamihiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Shimohiratsuka<ref name=yuzurizyou/> * Kizaki * Chōfukuji * Takō * Yaginuma }}
== Preservation as a National Historic Site == The following 11 locations were designated as part of the "Nitta-no-shō Iseki" National Historic Site: * Enpuku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Jūnisho Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Bessho Town) * Sōjiji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Chōraku-ji Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Tōshō-gū Precinct (Ōta City, Serada Town) * Myōō-in Precinct (Ōta City, An'yōji Town) * Ikushina Shrine Precinct (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Sorimachi Yakushi Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Sorimachi) * Edano Residence Ruins (Ōta City, Nitta Kamigoda Town) * Shigaden Water Source (Ōta City, Shinden Ichi no I Town) * Yatajin Water Source (Ōta City, Nitta Daikon Town) <ref name=yuzurizyou>「『新田義重譲状』正木文書2」新田義重は子・世良田義季とその母に譲る。大部分は世良田氏に伝領</ref> <ref name=nitaama>新田義兼後家「新田尼」より岩松氏へ伝領</ref> <ref name=raiouhaha>世良田義季母より本宗家へ伝領</ref>
